instructions:
Fix the FENCE_RE validator gap surfaced as TICKET-0086-V1 and produce a triage of the resulting drift. **Do NOT attempt to clean up the surfaced drift in this task** — the cleanup will be one or more separate batched tasks once we know the shape and scale.

## Background

0086's investigation confirmed that `FENCE_RE` in `ts-accuracy-validator.py` uses `^` with `re.MULTILINE`, which only matches column-0 backticks. Every indented fenced code block (4-space indent inside `<Tab>` / `<Tabs>` / `<CodeGroup>` / `<Accordion>` / etc components) has been silently skipped since doc-as-test launched. That's likely hundreds of code blocks across the multi-platform docs.

Flag-unflag-post.mdx:796 was one such miss (caught by manual grep in 0086, fixed there). The full scope is unknown until we fix the regex and run the gate.

## Part A — Apply the one-line fix

In `.docs-ops/validators/ts-accuracy-validator.py`, update `FENCE_RE` per TICKET-0086-V1:

```python
FENCE_RE = re.compile(r"^[ \t]*```([A-Za-z0-9_]+)[^\n]*\n(.*?)^[ \t]*```", re.MULTILINE | re.DOTALL)
```

The change: prepend `[ \t]*` to both opening and closing fence anchors so leading whitespace is accepted.

Run the validator's unit tests if any exist; if not, do a quick smoke test by running the validator directly on `social-plus-sdk/social/posts/flag-unflag-post.mdx` and confirming it now sees code blocks inside `<Tab>` (it would have skipped them before).

## Part B — Run the gate and capture impact

Run `python3 .docs-ops/ci/check-drift.py --json-out /tmp/drift-after-fence-fix.json` and capture the full delta.

Report in the triage doc:
  - **Total new (file, ref) pairs surfaced** by the fix
  - **Per-platform breakdown** (likely most are TS-only since this is the TS validator, but cohort grouping if useful)
  - **Per-file count** (sorted descending — top 20)
  - **Per-symbol top hits** (which API names appear most often in newly-scanned blocks) — this gives a sense of whether the new drift is "a few popular renames affecting many pages" vs "unique drift everywhere"
  - **Classification per top-hit**:
    - **REAL_DRIFT**: stale API name that needs a rewrite (like 0083's @hidden cleanups)
    - **NEEDS_SKIP_MARKER**: intentional illustrative snippet that should have a `doc-as-test: skip` marker (per CI_GATE.md §Opting out per block)
    - **VALIDATOR_FALSE_POSITIVE**: the validator misinterprets the block (rare — file as separate ticket if seen)

This triage doc IS the deliverable. Don't fix any of the drift.

## Part C — Update tickets

Mark `TICKET-0086-V1` as RESOLVED in `.docs-ops/evals/sdk-tickets-to-file.md` (with a reference to this task and a pointer to the triage doc for the resulting cleanup scope).

## Push policy for this branch

The gate will be RED after this task. That's expected and known. Note in the result that follow-up cleanup tasks will need to land before the branch can be merged. Do NOT bypass the gate (`--no-verify`) — the local pre-push hook will block, and that's fine for now since cleanup tasks are coming. Land this task's commits locally; the user (Claude) will handle the push-when-clean strategy.

## Out of scope

- Fixing any of the surfaced drift items (separate task(s) — to be designed based on this triage)
- Fixing IMPORT_RE (V2) — separate task 0088
- Adding skip markers — separate task driven by the NEEDS_SKIP_MARKER classification
- Touching the createUserToken page (C1) — needs SDK team input

## Success criteria

- `FENCE_RE` updated and validator's behavior verified on at least one previously-skipped block.
- `.docs-ops/evals/0087-fence-fix-triage.md` produced with all 5 sections (totals / per-platform / per-file top 20 / per-symbol top hits / classification of top hits).
- Triage classification covers AT LEAST the top 10 symbols by frequency (more is better).
- `TICKET-0086-V1` marked RESOLVED in `sdk-tickets-to-file.md`.
- The gate is allowed to go RED — `success_criteria.validators` below does NOT check drift gate verdict.

Report in `notes`: total drift count surfaced; whether the distribution is concentrated (few symbols, many files) or diffuse (many unique drifts); recommended structure for follow-up cleanup tasks (one big task vs split-by-platform vs split-by-symbol-cohort).

validators.name: fence_re_accepts_indented_blocks
validators.command: python3 -c "import re, sys; sys.path.insert(0, '.docs-ops/validators'); import importlib.util; spec=importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('v','.docs-ops/validators/ts-accuracy-validator.py'); m=importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(m); test='    \`\`\`typescript\n    const x = 1\n    \`\`\`'; matches=list(m.FENCE_RE.finditer(test)); assert len(matches)==1, f'FENCE_RE did not match indented block: {len(matches)} matches'; print(f'OK FENCE_RE matched indented block')"

validators.name: triage_doc_produced
validators.command: test -f .docs-ops/evals/0087-fence-fix-triage.md && wc -l .docs-ops/evals/0087-fence-fix-triage.md | awk '{ if ($1 > 30) print "OK triage doc has substance ("$1" lines)"; else { print "FAIL: triage doc too thin ("$1" lines)"; exit 1 } }'
